# Wellington Mota — Product Engineer, Design + Front-end & AI Specialist

> Product Engineer with 15+ years in digital product design and 3+ years shipping front-end in modern stacks. Specializes in closing the gap between design and code — from UX research and high-fidelity prototypes to production React/TypeScript, with no handoff. AI Specialist focused on agent orchestration, prompt engineering, evals, and RAG integration.

Based in Brasília, Brazil. Open to remote opportunities worldwide. Fluent in English and Portuguese.

---

## Professional Summary

Wellington Mota is a Product Engineer at the intersection of design systems, component architecture, and user-centered product thinking. He goes from UX research and high-fidelity prototypes to production React/TypeScript code, integrated with REST and GraphQL APIs.

With 15+ years in product design and 3+ years shipping front-end on modern stacks (React, Next.js, Node.js), he holds a degree in Systems Analysis and Development with an MBA in Fullstack in progress. He thrives on lean teams where one person can own the entire experience — cutting delivery time and handoff noise.

He uses AI beyond autocomplete: orchestrated agents, version-controlled prompts as code, and evals ensuring quality before deploy.

## Technical Skills

### Design
- User Research & Usability Testing
- Information Architecture
- Wireframes, User Flows, Interaction Design
- High-fidelity Prototyping (Figma)
- Design Systems: Tokens, Foundations, Component Libraries, Living Documentation
- Accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA)

### Front-end Engineering
- React, Next.js, TypeScript
- Node.js, Express
- Tailwind CSS, CSS-in-JS, CSS Modules
- REST API & GraphQL Integration
- Performance Optimization (Core Web Vitals)
- Responsive Design, Cross-browser Compatibility

### AI Engineering
- Agent Orchestration (MCP — Model Context Protocol)
- Prompt Engineering: Version-controlled, Tested Prompts
- Evals: LLM-as-judge, CI Quality Gates
- RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) in Product UI
- Token & Cost Optimization (Caching, Model Routing)
- Multi-agent Pipelines

### Tools & Platforms
- Figma, Storybook
- Git, GitHub, CI/CD
- Vercel, AWS
- Analytics, A/B Testing

## Services Offered

### 1. Product Design & UX (Strategic)
User research and usability testing, wireframes, user flows, information architecture, and high-fidelity prototypes. AI-enhanced: generates layout and copy variations with LLMs, synthesizes research data with automated analysis, accelerates A/B tests with evals.

### 2. Design Systems (Scalable)
Tokens and foundations, component libraries, living documentation. Used by 3+ teams. AI-enhanced: automates token generation, component documentation, and accessibility checklists with code agents.

### 3. Front-end Engineering (Reliable)
React, Next.js, TypeScript development. REST/GraphQL integration. Performance and accessibility optimization. AI-enhanced: agent-powered code review, assisted refactoring, test generation, automated visual regression detection.

### 4. AI Engineering & Orchestration (Advanced)
Agent orchestration via MCP, evals and CI quality gates, token and cost optimization, RAG in product UI. Multi-agent pipelines, version-controlled prompts, smart caching, LLM-as-judge evals in CI.

### 5. Rapid Prototyping — 0 to 1 (Fast)
MVP in days not months, from concept to clickable, validation with real users. AI-enhanced: full project scaffolding with agents, complete flow prototyping in hours, real-time iteration.

## Work Process

1. **Discovery** (25%): Understand business, audience, goals. User research, benchmarking, product direction. AI: Interview synthesis and competitive analysis with LLMs.

2. **Design** (65%): Information architecture, design system, high-fidelity screens. Rapid iteration with usability testing. AI: Layout and copy variations generated in minutes.

3. **Development** (90%): Design to responsive, performant React/TypeScript code integrated with APIs — no handoff. AI: Code agents for scaffolding, testing, and code review.

4. **Delivery & Metrics** (100%): Testing, final adjustments, monitoring with analytics and A/B testing. AI: Automated evals and continuous monitoring with agents.

## FAQ

**Do you really do both design and development?**
Yes. I own both sides: UX research and high-fidelity design, plus React/TypeScript implementation in production. This eliminates the handoff and drastically reduces delivery time.

**Do you work remotely?**
Yes. Based in Brasília, Brazil, and open to fully remote opportunities, including international — fluent English, with experience serving clients in the UK and US.

**What does your workflow look like?**
Discovery → Design → Development → Delivery. With feedback checkpoints at each stage and metrics (analytics, A/B testing) to validate product impact.

**What technologies do you work with?**
React, Next.js, TypeScript, Node.js, Tailwind, and REST/GraphQL integration on the dev side; Figma, design systems, and usability research on the design side.

**How do you use AI in your work?**
Far beyond code autocomplete. I orchestrate agents and multi-step flows (with MCP), version and test prompts as code, optimize cost/tokens (caching and model routing), and use LLM-as-judge evals to ensure quality before deploy — integrating AI features directly into the product UI.

**Are you a "vibe coder"?**
No. "Vibe coding" means requesting code and hoping it works. I do Design Engineering: I design the experience and implement it with production discipline — version-controlled and tested prompts, evals with CI gates before deploy, cost and token control, design system as single source of truth, accessibility and performance. AI is a tool under engineering, not a crutch.
